hypothesis: Weighting DPO preference pairs by a Thermo-Calc–derived confidence metric (e.g., database coverage and phase-prediction variance) such that higher-confidence evaluations receive larger preference weights will increase mean reward and BCC/B2 objective-satisfaction rates and reduce forward DKL(DPO ∥ SFT) on domain-critical tokens—thereby preventing post-DPO performance collapse in brittle architectures like OLMo—relative to standard unweighted DPO.
